faqs.question: What's the best caravan park in Ballina?
faqs.answer: BIG4 Tasman Holiday Parks - Ballina consistently tops the list for families, with brilliant water play areas and jumping pillows that keep the kids entertained for hours. Discovery Parks - Ballina is another cracker choice with spacious sites, while both Reflections parks (Shaws Bay and Ballina) offer unbeatable riverside locations perfect for kayaking and fishing right from your doorstep.

faqs.question: What's the best caravan park in Ballina NSW for families?
faqs.answer: You can't go wrong with BIG4 Tasman Holiday Parks - Ballina if you've got little ones—the facilities are top-notch and there's plenty to keep them busy on-site. Reflections Shaws Bay Holiday Park is brilliant if you prefer a quieter, nature-focused stay with direct river access, while Discovery Parks - Ballina strikes a nice balance between amenities and that relaxed coastal atmosphere families love.

faqs.question: What are the best kids activities in Ballina?
faqs.answer: Shelly Beach is perfect for swimming and rock pool exploring, while the Richmond River breakwall is legendary for dolphin spotting—pack some fish and chips for a sunset picnic. The Big Prawn is a must-do photo op, and don't miss grabbing a gelato at Angels Beach. For rainy days, the Ballina Naval and Maritime Museum has fascinating exhibits that even keep teenagers interested.

faqs.question: What's a good kids day out in Ballina?
faqs.answer: Start at Shelly Beach for a morning swim and rock pool ramble, then grab lunch at one of the cafes near Angels Beach. Afternoon is perfect for heading to Missingham Lookout to spot dolphins or whales (September to November), followed by fish and chips at the Richmond River breakwall. Finish with a play at one of the riverside parks—it's a proper Aussie beach day the whole family will love.

faqs.question: Where can I find kids therapy services in Ballina?
faqs.answer: Ballina has several excellent paediatric therapy services including speech pathology, occupational therapy, and physiotherapy. The Ballina Community Health Centre on Cherry Street is a great starting point, and there are private practitioners throughout town. It's worth checking with your GP for specific recommendations and referrals, or contacting the North Coast Primary Health Network for a comprehensive list of local providers and their specialisations.

faqs.question: What age do toddlers start throwing a ball?
faqs.answer: Most toddlers start attempting to throw a ball around 18 months, though it's more of a cute forward fling than an actual throw! By age two, they'll usually manage a proper overhead throw, and by three they're getting pretty decent aim. Every child develops differently though, so don't stress if your little one takes a bit longer—just keep practising at the park or beach and they'll get there in their own time.

faqs.question: What can you do in Ballina with toddlers?
faqs.answer: Shelly Beach is absolutely brilliant for toddlers—the water's gentle and there are shallow rock pools perfect for little explorers. Angels Beach has a fantastic playground right near the sand, and the flat riverside paths are perfect for scooters and bikes. Pop by the Big Prawn for photos, and grab a babyccino at one of the family-friendly cafes. Most holiday parks like BIG4 Tasman have toddler-friendly splash pads too.
